title: narcissus poeticus subsp. verbanensis
intro: Narcissus poeticus subsp. verbanensis, also known as the Poet's Narcissus, is a subspecies of the flowering plant Narcissus poeticus, which is a perennial bulbous plant that grows from 15 to 30 cm in height and produces white, waxy, and highly fragrant flowers. The medicinal properties of Narcissus poeticus subsp. verbanensis include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as colds, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of consuming or using this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, boost the immune system, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as coughs and congestion. Traditional herbal preparations of Narcissus poeticus subsp. verbanensis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, tonics, and salves for various health purposes.
